column1: 〜かいがある / 〜かいがあって (worth the effort)
column2:
The effort put in was rewarded; it was worth doing.
Connection: Vる / Vた + かいがある / かいがあって
Ex: 努力のかいあって、希望の大学に合格した。 — My efforts were rewarded because I got into the university I wanted to.
この年まで待ったかいがあった。 — It was worth waiting until this age (to marry).

column1: 〜かいもなく (despite the effort, in vain)
column2:
Despite putting in effort, the result was not achieved; in vain.
Connection: Vる / Vた + かいもなく
Ex: 手術のかいもなく、愛犬が死んでしまった。 — Even though my pet dog had an operation, he died.
一生懸命練習したかいがなかった。(予選で落ちた) — My hard practice was in vain (I failed the prelims).

Used to express that something is worth the effort, value, or effect of doing it.
Ex: やりがいのある仕事 — Work worth doing.
教えがいのある生徒 — Students worth teaching.

column1: 〜てまで / 〜までして (even if it means...)
column2:
To do something extreme or surprising for a purpose.
Connection: Vて + まで(も) / N + まで + Vて
Ex: 親にうそをついてまで遊びに行きたくない。 — I don't want to go if I have to lie to my parents.
いやな思いをしてまで、彼女と付き合うことないよ。 — There is no reason to keep going out with her if you don't enjoy it.

Do something while a state or condition still exists; before it changes.
Connection: Vる / Vない / Vている / Aい / naな / Nの + うちに
Ex: 忘れないうちに、メモしておこう。 — I will make a note before I forget.
桜がきれいなうちに、お花見に行きましょう。 — Let's go to see the cherry blossoms while they are still beautiful.

column1: 〜か / 〜ないかのうちに (as soon as / just as)
column2:
Something happens almost simultaneously with another action.
Connection: Vる + か + Vない + かの + うちに
Ex: 授業が終わるか終わらないかのうちに、彼は教室を出た。 — As soon as class ended, he left the classroom.
布団に入るか入らないかのうちに眠ってしまう。 — I fell fast asleep the moment I got under the covers.

column1: 〜限り (as long as / to the limit of)
column2:
As long as a certain condition continues; or within the scope of one's knowledge/abilities.
Connection: Vる / Vない / Vている / Aい / Aくない / naな / naである / Nである + 限り
Ex: 日本にいる限り、日本語は必要だ。 — As long as you are in Japan, you need Japanese.
私の調べた限りでは、この研究はだれもしていない。 — To the best of my research, no one has done this study.

column1: 〜さえ / 〜さえすれば (if only / just by)
column2:
If only X were the case; just doing X is enough.
Connection: N + さえ / Vば / Aければ / naなら + さえすれば
Ex: 携帯電話さえあれば、時計もカメラもいらない。 — As long as I have a cell phone, I don't need a watch or camera.
この薬を飲みさえすれば、すぐに治ります。 — Just by taking this medicine, you will recover immediately.

Emphasizing that a reason is the true or specific reason.
Connection: V / A / na / N + からこそ
Ex: 子どもがかわいいからこそ、しかるんです。 — I scold my child precisely because I love him/her.
努力したからこそ、成功したんです。 — I succeeded precisely because I worked hard.

Emphasizing that a certain state is a prerequisite for something else to happen.
Connection: Vて + こそ
Ex: お互いに信頼してこそ、悩みも言える。 — Only when we trust each other can we talk about our problems.
親になってこそ、親の苦労がわかる。 — Only after becoming a parent do you understand the hardships of parents.

column1: 〜にしたら / 〜にすれば (from the perspective of)
column2:
Taking the perspective of a specific person or group.
Connection: N + にしたら / にすれば
Ex: 子どもにしたら、親の期待は苦痛だ。 — From the child's perspective, parental expectations are painful.

column1: 〜としたら / 〜とすれば (if we assume)
column2:
Hypothesizing a condition; "if".
Connection: V / A / na / N + としたら / とすれば
Ex: それが本当だとしたら、夢のようです。 — If that is true, it is like a dream.

Even if a certain assumption is true.
Connection: V / A / na / N + としても
Ex: 行くとしても、旅行者としてしか行けない。 — Even if I go, I can only go as a tourist.

column1: 〜とともに (together with / at the same time as)
column2:
Two things happening simultaneously; or one change causing another.
Connection: N + とともに / Vる + とともに
Ex: 家族とともに来日した。 — I came to Japan together with my family.
自動車が普及するとともに、事故も増加した。 — As cars became popular, accidents increased as well.

As X happens, Y also happens (often causal).
Connection: N + にともない / Vる + にともなって
Ex: 人口の増加にともない、資源の消費量も増える。 — As population increases, resource consumption increases.

A gradual, progressive change occurring in parallel with another.
Connection: N + につれて / Vる + につれて
Ex: 年を取るにつれて、体の機能が低下する。 — As we get older, body functions deteriorate.

column1: 〜にしたがって (in accordance with / as)
column2:
As X changes, Y changes; or following a rule/instruction.
Connection: N + にしたがって / Vる + にしたがって
Ex: 北へ行くにしたがって、紅叶は早くなる。 — As you go north, the autumn colors come earlier.
予定表にしたがい、学習を進める。 — I study according to the schedule.
the time at which a performance/show actually starts
the time the venue/hall opens its doors, before the show starts
unreserved/free seating — sit wherever you like
an advance-purchase ticket, bought before the day of the event (usually cheaper)
a ticket bought on the day of the event itself (usually pricier than advance)
winning a prize / placing in a competition
a water outage / suspension of the water supply
water-supply (pipe) construction work
water drawn and stored in advance, in case of an outage
reddish, cloudy tap water, which can occur right after water-main work
the water utility department, the municipal office in charge of the water supply
a day trip — leaving and returning the same day, with no overnight stay
the time everyone is supposed to gather/meet before departure
a participation fee
the maximum capacity, the greatest number of people allowed
to close applications/registration, once full or by a deadline
delivery of goods to the customer
the delivery deadline / due date
a delay
goods arriving in stock at a store or warehouse
making arrangements for something to be done
Dear ... — the formal opening greeting of a Japanese letter
Sincerely — the formal closing of a Japanese letter, pairs with 拝啓
screening/review of application documents, the first stage of hiring
being hired/accepted for a job
being rejected for a job, not hired
a formal demand-for-payment notice
legal action/measures
a mix-up where two things (e.g. a payment and a notice) cross paths, each unaware of the other
to press/urge someone, e.g. to pay or respond
a payment being made / money coming in
to run into / meet someone by chance
casual contraction of 置いていった ("left [it] behind and went")
casual contraction of なくなってしまった ("ended up gone/missing")
= チェックする — to check/confirm
= 用意する — to prepare/get ready
= 頼む — to order (food, a service)
= 直す — to correct/fix
= 捨てる — to dispose of/throw away
the most / number one — flags the speaker's top priority in a listening passage
most — a more formal equivalent of 一番
more than anything — emphasizes the single most important thing
especially/particularly
actually / in truth — signals the real, often contrasting, fact
actually / to tell the truth — introduces a hidden or surprising fact
a wedding reception
to ask someone to give a speech
to discuss/consult with someone about a decision
choosing an apartment/condo
a course/route/plan option, e.g. a tour package
one's wish/preference

column1: 重要ではない言葉に惑わされない
column2: a listening strategy: don't be misled/distracted by unimportant words
